preamble.summary: Judah's sin engraved / the heart is deceitful. LXX OMITS vv. 1-4 entirely. This is a significant omission — four full verses about Judah's sin being written with an iron pen and engraved on the tablet of their heart.
preamble.notable_variants: LXX LACKS vv. 1-4 completely. The LXX chapter begins at what is MT v. 5 ('Cursed is the man who trusts in man'). The omitted material includes the famous 'iron pen' / 'diamond point' imagery and the reference to Asherim and high places. Some scholars see these verses as a later MT expansion drawn from other Jeremianic passages.
preamble.structural_notes: LXX chapter 17 begins at MT v. 5. LXX vv. 1-4 correspond to MT vv. 5-8 etc. The numbering offset means LXX 17:1 = MT 17:5.

verses.mt_reading: חַטַּאת יְהוּדָה כְּתוּבָה בְּעֵט בַּרְזֶל בְּצִפֹּרֶן שָׁמִיר חֲרוּשָׁה עַל־לוּחַ לִבָּם וּלְקַרְנוֹת מִזְבְּחוֹתֵיכֶם
verses.mt_rendering: The sin of Judah is written with a pen of iron, with a point of diamond; it is engraved upon the tablet of their heart and upon the horns of your altars.
variant_notes: ENTIRELY ABSENT from LXX. This famous verse about sin inscribed with an iron pen is not in the Greek tradition. The imagery may have been added to the MT from other prophetic sources.

verses.mt_reading: כִּזְכֹּר בְּנֵיהֶם מִזְבְּחוֹתָם וַאֲשֵׁרֵיהֶם עַל־עֵץ רַעֲנָן עַל גְּבָעוֹת הַגְּבֹהוֹת
verses.mt_rendering: As their children remember their altars and their Asherim beside every green tree upon the high hills.
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX. The reference to Asherim and high-place worship is not in the Greek.

verses.mt_reading: הֲרָרִי בַּשָּׂדֶה חֵילְךָ כָל־אוֹצְרוֹתֶיךָ לָבַז אֶתֵּן בָּמוֹתֶיךָ בְּחַטָּאת בְּכָל־גְּבוּלֶיךָ
verses.mt_rendering: O My mountain in the field, I will give your wealth and all your treasures for spoil — your high places for sin throughout all your borders.
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX.

verses.mt_reading: וְשָׁמַטְתָּה וּבְךָ מִנַּחֲלָתְךָ אֲשֶׁר נָתַתִּי לָךְ וְהַעֲבַדְתִּיךָ אֶת־אֹיְבֶיךָ בָּאָרֶץ אֲשֶׁר לֹא־יָדָעְתָּ
verses.mt_rendering: And you shall let go, even of yourself, from your heritage which I gave you; and I will cause you to serve your enemies in a land which you do not know.
variant_notes: ABSENT from LXX. The entire four-verse unit (1-4) is missing from the Greek, and the chapter opens instead with the 'cursed/blessed' contrast.
LXX and MT are substantially identical here.
